evals.prompt: Run a Quick Check on the skill at evals/files/healthy-skill/ (core file is FIXTURE.md)
evals.expected_output: A report showing the skill is HEALTHY or MOSTLY HEALTHY with no critical or failed tests. The script should pass syntax validation, cross-references should match, and best practices should score well.
expectations: Report contains a summary card with a verdict of HEALTHY or MOSTLY HEALTHY
Script syntax validation passes for extract_key_points.py
Cross-reference consistency passes — FIXTURE.md uses --file which matches the script
Frontmatter completeness passes — name and description are present
No security findings at Warning or Critical severity
Best practices score is 17/22 or higher
Description quality is flagged as having good negative guidance (when NOT to use)

evals.prompt: Run a Quick Check on the skill at evals/files/broken-skill/ (core file is FIXTURE.md)
evals.expected_output: A report showing NEEDS ATTENTION or worse, with cross-reference mismatches, a missing reference file, and a constraint contradiction detected.
expectations: Cross-reference consistency fails — FIXTURE.md uses --input/--output/--format but script expects --file/--out/--fmt
Reference integrity fails — references/style-guide.md is referenced but does not exist
Constraint consistency fails — 'ALWAYS include headers' contradicts 'NEVER include headers when format is JSON'
Report lists all three argument mismatches individually
Improvement suggestions include fixing the argument names
Description quality is flagged as weak — no trigger phrases, no negative guidance

evals.prompt: Run SkillCheck on the skill at evals/files/incomplete-skill/ (core file is FIXTURE.md)
evals.expected_output: SkillCheck detects the skill is incomplete and produces a progress report instead of a standard failure report. It should identify what exists, what's missing, and suggest next steps.
expectations: SkillCheck identifies the skill as incomplete (detects TODO markers and placeholder text)
Output is framed as a progress report, not a standard test report
Reports that scripts/build_report.py is referenced but does not exist
Reports that the description is a placeholder (TODO)
Reports that output format is not specified ([fill in] placeholder)
Suggests concrete next steps in priority order
Does not use the standard verdict scale (HEALTHY/CRITICAL) — uses IN PROGRESS or similar
Defaults to Quick Check depth even if Standard was requested

evals.prompt: Run a Quick Check on the skill at evals/files/insecure-skill/ (core file is FIXTURE.md)
evals.expected_output: A report with multiple Critical security findings. The script contains shell injection via os.system, eval() on user data, a hardcoded API key, disabled SSL verification, and writes to /etc/.
expectations: Security review flags os.system with user input as Critical (shell injection)
Security review flags eval() usage as Critical (arbitrary code execution)
Security review flags the hardcoded API key as Critical (credential exposure)
Security review flags verify=False as Warning (disabled SSL)
Security review flags writing to /etc/ as Warning (sensitive path)
FIXTURE.md instruction security flags 'execute user-provided command' as a risk
Report prominently warns against distributing this skill before fixing security issues
Improvement suggestions list security fixes as Critical priority

evals.prompt: Run a Standard Check on the skill at evals/files/healthy-skill/ (core file is FIXTURE.md)
evals.expected_output: A full Standard report including script execution tests and I/O contract validation. The script should run successfully with mock input and produce expected output.
expectations: All Quick Check tests pass
Script execution test passes — extract_key_points.py runs with mock .txt input
Mock input file is generated as a .txt file with realistic paragraph content
Script produces bullet-point output when given valid input
Missing argument test passes — script fails gracefully without --file
Report notes that no existing evals were found and suggests adding them
Report includes a depth note recommending Deep Check for edge cases
